decisions.summary: Implement the migration inside resumeAgent() (src/lib/agents.ts:4655), not deacon-only code. The deacon calls resumeAgent(agentId) bare (deacon.ts:6434), so the deacon path is covered, and manual no-harness resumes of legacy agents migrate identically. Caller-explicit opts.harness bypasses the migration.

Legacy predicate = runtime snapshot lacks sessionModel or sessionHarness (same predicate sessionResumeDriftReasons bails on at agents.ts:2164). Comparison baseline = agentState.harness ?? 'claude-code' (pre-PAN-636 states ran the old claude-code launcher default).

Fold the migration into the existing drift machinery: push a 'legacy harness migration old→new' reason onto resumeDriftReasons (agents.ts:4668) so ALL downstream fresh-session plumbing (fresh session id, session.id unlink, defaultResumeMessage, kickoff-style delivery, fresh-respawn logging) behaves exactly like PAN-1787 drift. Plus one dedicated migration log line (console + logAgentLifecycleSync).

Fix the origin-write gap for ALL resumeAgent fresh respawns (drift, compact, migration): extend the saveAgentRuntimeState call at agents.ts:4807-4810 with sessionModel/sessionHarness when !shouldResumeSavedSession. Without this, the reducer's '?? prev' semantics (event-reducers.ts:1088-1096) keep stale origin and a drifted agent fresh-respawns on EVERY subsequent resume — the issue's 'one-time by construction' claim depends on this write.
restartAgent stays unchanged (NonGoal): restarts are operator-initiated and can pass --harness; the issue targets resume.
No config flag/kill-switch for the migration — it is one-time by construction and a flag would outlive its purpose.

Test strategy: integration tests in tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts using stub pi/codex binaries on PATH (established pattern at line 917-923) + resetHarnessResolveCachesForTests(); unit tests in src/lib/__tests__/agents-spawn-supervisor.test.ts using the existing resolveHarnessMock/emitAgentEventMock patterns (lines 331-420).

hazards.summary: Missing pi/codex binary at resolution time makes resolveHarness fall back to claude-code (harness-resolve.ts:99-104), which usually equals the stored harness → no migration on that machine.
hazards.mitigation: Correct by design — never migrate onto an uninstalled harness. Tests must stub binaries on PATH and reset the availability cache to stay deterministic (NFR-2).

hazards.summary: The reducer keeps prior origin values (sessionModel ?? prev.sessionModel), so emitting only ONE of sessionModel/sessionHarness would half-update origin.
hazards.mitigation: WI-2 writes both fields together in a single saveAgentRuntimeState patch; WI-4 asserts both fields on the emitted model_set event.

hazards.summary: First restart after landing triggers a fleet-wide wave of fresh sessions for every agent whose pairing changes (operator-confirmed and desired).
hazards.mitigation: Deacon concurrency/load gates already stagger resumes (deacon.ts:6413-6428); fresh sessions get the standard continue.json re-onboarding prompt; one migration log line per agent provides the audit trail.

hazards.summary: Behavior change for legacy agents whose stored harness is policy-denied: today the explicit resolveHarness call throws and the resume fails; the migration path (no explicit) falls back to claude-code and may launch a fresh session.
hazards.mitigation: Strictly an improvement (a permanently-failing resume becomes a working migration). Documented in the PRD; no extra code needed.

hazards.summary: The existing PAN-1787 backward-compat test ('keeps --resume for legacy sessions with no origin metadata', tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts:621) must KEEP passing — its scenario (anthropic model + claude-code) re-resolves to claude-code, the equal case.
hazards.mitigation: WI-3 AC explicitly requires that test to pass unmodified; any change to it is a red flag that the migration fired on the equal case.

hazards.summary: Harness availability results are cached per-harness in a module-level map (harness-resolve.ts:14), so a PATH change mid-test is invisible without a cache reset.
hazards.mitigation: Call resetHarnessResolveCachesForTests() immediately after any in-test PATH mutation (pattern already at agent-spawning.test.ts:922-923).

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session for PAN-1797 (auto mode). Verified PAN-1787 resume/drift machinery, found the resumeAgent origin-write gap, produced PRD draft + vBRIEF.
